data_description.host: Hostname or IP of the add-on (default: http://127.0.0.1 under Supervisor).
data_description.port: TCP port the add-on is listening on (default 8099).

hassio_confirm.title: HA MCP Bridge discovered
hassio_confirm.description: Set up Home Assistant to connect to the discovered add-on: **{addon}**?

mcp_setup.title: Connect to an MCP Server (optional)
mcp_setup.description:
Optionally provide an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server URL. Leave blank to skip — you can configure this later in the integration options.

Examples:
- HA built-in: `http://homeassistant.local:8123/api/mcp_server/sse`
- External proxy: `https://my-mcp.example.com/sse`

data_description.mcp_url: Full URL of the MCP Server SSE endpoint. Leave blank to skip.
data_description.mcp_token: Bearer token for authentication. Required for most external servers; optional for HA's built-in MCP.

error.cannot_connect: Unable to reach the add-on. Make sure it is installed and running.
error.cannot_connect_mcp: Unable to reach the MCP server at the provided URL. Check the URL and token, or leave blank to skip.

abort.cannot_connect: Could not reach the add-on. Start it and try again from Settings → Devices & Services.

data_description.update_interval: How often HA polls the add-on and MCP server. Min 10 s, max 3600 s.
data_description.mcp_url: Leave blank to disable MCP server monitoring.
data_description.mcp_token: Bearer token for the MCP server.
